uk: Святої мучениці Софії і трьох її дочок: Віри, Надії й Любові.
saints.en: St. Martyr Sophia and her three daughters: Faith, Hope & Love

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма рядова. На «Господи взиваю» – 6 стихир: 3 свята і 3 мчц. Слава: мчц., І нині: свята. Прокімен дня. На стиховні: стихири свята. Тропар мчц., Слава, І нині: свята. Відпуст середній з помин. служби свята і мчц.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар свята (2), Слава: мчц., І нині: свята. Катизми чергові і сідальний свята. Псалом 50. Канон свята і мчц. По 9-ій – світильний мчц. Слава, І нині: світильний свята. Стиховня – вся свята. Тропар мчц., Слава, І нині: свята.
Часи: тропар свята, також на 3-му і 9-му ще Слава: мчц., кондаки – поперемінно, тобто: на 1-му і 6-му – свята, на 3-му і 9-му – мчц.
Літургія: антифони перших два – з самого свята, третій – загальний з приспівом свята, також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся» з приспівом свята. Тропар свята, Слава, І нині: кондак свята. Прокімен, алилуя і причасний – свята, апостол і євангелія – дня. Ап. – Еф. 225 зач.; 4, 14-19. Єв. – Мр. 52 зач.; 11, 27-33. Замість «Достойно» – ірмос 9-ої пісні канону свята з його приспівом.

troparion_en.text: You blossomed in the courts of the Lord / as a fruitful olive tree, / holy martyr Sophia; / in your contest you offered to Christ the sweet fruit of your womb, / your daughters Faith, Hope, and Love. / Together with them intercede for us all.

kontakion_en.text: The holy branches of noble Sophia, / Faith, Hope, and Love, / confounded Greek sophistry through Grace. / They struggled and won the victory / and have been granted an incorruptible crown by Christ the Master of all.

matched_oca_saint: Martyr Sophia and her three daughters at Rome

oca_saints.name: Afterfeast of the Elevation of the Cross

troparion.text: O Lord, save Your people, / and bless Your inheritance. / Grant victories to the Orthodox Christians / over their adversaries. / And by virtue of Your Cross, / preserve Your habitation.

kontakion.text: As You were voluntarily raised upon the cross for our sake, / grant mercy to those who are called by Your Name, O Christ God; / make all Orthodox Christians glad by Your power, / granting them victories over their adversaries, / by bestowing on them the Invincible trophy, Your weapon of Peace.

oca_saints.name: Martyr Sophia and her three daughters at Rome

troparion.text: You blossomed in the courts of the Lord / as a fruitful olive tree, / holy martyr Sophia; / in your contest you offered to Christ the sweet fruit of your womb, / your daughters Faith, Hope, and Love. / Together with them intercede for us all.

kontakion.text: The holy branches of noble Sophia, / Faith, Hope, and Love, / confounded Greek sophistry through Grace. / They struggled and won the victory / and have been granted an incorruptible crown by Christ the Master of all.

troparion.text: Your lamb Theodota calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice: / "I love You, my Bridegroom, and in seeking You I endure suffering. / In baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You, / and I died so that I might live with You. / Accept me as a pure sacrifice, / for I have offered myself in love." / Through her prayers save our souls, since You are merciful.

Your lamb Agathocleia calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice: / "I love You, my Bridegroom, and in seeking You I endure suffering. / In baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You, / and I died so that I might live with You. / Accept me as a pure sacrifice, / for I have offered myself in love." / Through her prayers save our souls, since You are merciful.

oca_saints.name: 156 Martyrs of Palestine, including Bishops Peleus and Nilus, the Presbyter Zeno, the Noblemen Patermuthius and Elias, and others

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Constantinople Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Your icon, O Sovereign Lady, has shone for us like a sun shining from the east,  / with a wondrous radiance of miracles, /  illuminating all those who come to it with faith and love, /  praying fervently to your majesty, in your Son and God. /  Glory to God, Who gave us this Icon with the help of Saint Euphrosynos.  / Glory to Him Who brought it from Constantinople.  / Glory to Him, Who through it, grants healing to all.

kontakion.text: The most glorious Mother of the Almighty  / summons us to sing with the coming of her Icon from Constantinople, /  and inspires us to fight in order to overcome the power of those who oppose God,  / and to praise her in song as the cause of our joy: /  “Rejoice, O Bride who knew not wedlock.”
